3.happening: The returned values start as an empty list.
3.why: The linked list will be reversed first, then read out into this list.
3.invariant: result contains only values already visited in the final reversed traversal.

4.happening: The input values are copied into node storage.
4.why: Each index now represents one linked-list node in the visualization.
4.invariant: Node values do not change during reversal.

5.why: The number of nodes determines how many next pointers are needed.
5.invariant: The algorithm never creates or deletes nodes.

6.why: -1 is the no-node sentinel, which is correct for the tail and safe for empty lists.
6.invariant: Every link is either -1 or a valid node ID.

7.why: The original forward links are built from the first node to the last.
7.invariant: All links before i have already been assigned.

8.happening: Node {i} is not the last node.
8.why: It should point to the node immediately after it.
8.invariant: The original linked list is being reconstructed one next pointer at a time.

8.happening: The original forward links are complete.
8.why: The tail keeps -1, so there is no more link to build.
8.invariant: The visual linked list now matches the input order.

9.why: This creates the original next arrow.
9.invariant: Prepared nodes point to their original next nodes.

10.why: The next node's original link can now be prepared.
10.invariant: No reversed links have been made yet.

11.why: Before the head is processed, the reversed part is empty.
11.invariant: previous points to the head of the already reversed part.

12.why: The reversal begins at the original head.
12.invariant: current points to the next node that still needs its link reversed.

13.why: There is no node 0, so the reversal loop must not run.
13.invariant: An empty linked list reverses to an empty linked list.

13.why: Node 0 is a valid first node to process.
13.invariant: current can safely begin at the original head.

14.why: This marks that there is no node to process.
14.invariant: The reversal loop will skip empty input safely.

15.why: nxt will temporarily remember the original next node before each link is changed.
15.invariant: nxt is only a saved pointer; it does not change the list by itself.

16.happening: No link is marked as changed yet.
16.why: The reversal has not rewritten any next pointer.
16.invariant: All original links are still intact.

17.happening: current is node {current}, so one more link must be reversed.
17.why: Each loop iteration reverses exactly one node's next pointer.
17.invariant: previous heads the reversed prefix; current heads the unreversed suffix.

17.happening: current is -1, so every node has been processed.
17.why: There is no unreversed suffix left.
17.invariant: previous now points to the new head of the fully reversed list.

18.happening: The original next pointer of current is saved in nxt.
18.why: Once current.next is overwritten, this saved pointer is the only way to continue forward.
18.invariant: nxt points to the remaining unreversed suffix.

19.happening: Node {current} is marked as the link that will change.
19.why: The animation can now emphasize the exact arrow being reversed.
19.invariant: Only current's next pointer is about to be rewritten.

20.happening: current.next is about to point back to previous.
20.why: This is the core reversal step: the arrow is flipped from forward to backward.
20.invariant: After this update, current becomes part of the reversed prefix.

21.why: current is now the first node of the reversed prefix.
21.invariant: previous always points to the head of the reversed portion.

22.why: The algorithm continues with the next original node.
22.invariant: The unreversed suffix begins at current.

23.why: The pointer-reversal loop is done, so no more arrows are being rewritten.
23.invariant: The links now form the reversed list.

24.happening: current is set to previous, the new head.
24.why: The final traversal should start at the head of the reversed list.
24.invariant: previous holds the new head after all reversals.

25.happening: current points to node {current} in the reversed list.
25.why: Its value is the next output value.
25.invariant: result contains the reversed values already read.

25.why: The final traversal has passed the tail.
25.invariant: result now contains the complete reversed list.

26.happening: The value at node {current} is read.
26.why: The final answer is represented as a list of node values.
26.invariant: The linked structure is no longer changing.

27.why: This records one more node from the reversed list.
27.invariant: The output order follows the reversed next pointers.

28.happening: current follows the reversed next pointer.
28.why: This moves the readout traversal toward the tail.
28.invariant: Every visited node contributes exactly one value.

29.why: All node links have been reversed and all values have been read.
29.invariant: The returned list is the original list in reverse order.

init.happening: Set up the linked-list representation and pointer variables.
init.why: The algorithm needs current, previous, and next pointers to reverse links safely.

loop.happening: Check whether another node needs processing.
loop.why: The loop stops when current reaches the no-node sentinel.
loop.invariant: previous is the reversed prefix and current is the unreversed suffix.

update.happening: Rewrite or record the current step's state.
update.why: Reversal happens by changing one next pointer at a time.

move.happening: Move one of the traversal pointers.
move.why: Saving nxt, then advancing previous and current, keeps the remaining list reachable.

check.why: An empty list has no head node to process.
check.invariant: current is valid or safely set to -1.

return.why: The reversed linked list has been fully traversed.
return.invariant: The output contains every original value exactly once.
